title: Scaffold shape module topology and core data contracts
description:
Implementation Scope:
Create/align module layout to `docs/PLAN.md` (`cli`, `csv`, `checks`, `scan`, `normalize`, `format`, `output`, `refusal`, orchestrator entrypoints) and define core structs/enums needed for pipeline wiring.

Deliverables:
- Rust modules and `mod.rs` declarations compile cleanly.
- Shared data structures defined for parsed inputs, scan results, checks, outcomes, and refusal payload envelopes.
- Contracts are minimal but sufficient for parallel lane development.

Definition of Done:
- `cargo check --all-targets` passes with scaffolded contracts.
- No placeholder API contradicts PLAN field names or semantics.
- Other lane tasks can import stable types without churn.

Test Cases:
- Compile-time smoke test via `cargo check`.
- Basic constructor/serde tests for top-level model structs where applicable.
acceptance_criteria: 1) cargo check all targets passes after scaffold changes.\n2) Module tree matches PLAN namespaces with no missing mod wiring.\n3) Core pipeline structs and enums are importable by downstream modules.

title: Implement human renderer and formatting helpers
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement human-mode rendering with fixed layout blocks, numeric formatting rules, dialect formatting, reason listing, key-line conditionality, and refusal-specific header form.

Deliverables:
- Human renderer for all outcomes.
- Formatting helpers for comma-separated counts and percentage display.
- Identifier encoding integration (`u8:`/`hex:`) where required in JSON path and reason contexts.

Definition of Done:
- Output layout/order matches PLAN exactly.
- Conditional sections appear only in allowed scenarios.
- Refusal mode includes gathered context and concrete `Next:` guidance.

Test Cases:
- Golden text snapshots for key/no-key compatible outputs.
- Incompatible snapshots for type shift and key viability failures.
- Refusal snapshots for `E_EMPTY`, `E_DIALECT`, and early file errors.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Human output layout and section ordering match PLAN templates.\n2) Number formatting and dialect rendering follow specified conventions.\n3) Snapshot tests verify key, no-key, incompatible, and refusal variants.

title: Resolve cross-lane interface mismatches to green compile
description:
Implementation Scope:
Execute the first integration cut across all core lanes to achieve a clean compile-time interface boundary. This task is strictly about wiring and contracts, not deep regression debugging.

Deliverables:
- Resolve cross-lane type and API mismatches after merging core lane outputs.
- Eliminate temporary glue and interface duplication introduced during parallel work.
- Produce a stable integrated build that downstream hardening can test.

Definition of Done:
- `cargo check --all-targets` passes on integrated lane output.
- Core orchestrator, checks, csv, and renderers compile together without adapter hacks.
- Remaining work (if any) is limited to behavioral regressions, not compile/interface failures.

Test Cases:
- Integration compile smoke run across full workspace targets.
- Minimal end-to-end invocation build check for key and no-key flows.
- Sanity check that refusal and compatible paths both compile through final wiring.
acceptance_criteria: 1) cargo check all targets passes after cross-lane merge.\n2) Core module boundaries compile without temporary adapter layers.\n3) Remaining follow-up, if any, is runtime behavior hardening rather than compile mismatches.

title: Wire orchestrator output rendering and final payload handoff
description:
Implementation Scope:
Split final orchestration concerns from core fail-fast pipeline logic. This task owns selecting human/json renderer, constructing final output payload strings, and preserving refusal context through final handoff.

Deliverables:
- Integrate orchestrator domain result with json and human renderers.
- Ensure partial-context refusal rendering preserves known dialect and file context.
- Keep run() handoff contract stable for stream routing.

Definition of Done:
- Renderer selection logic is centralized and deterministic.
- Final payload creation does not alter check or refusal semantics from upstream pipeline logic.
- Integration tests verify compatible, incompatible, and refusal rendering through orchestrator handoff.

Test Cases:
- End-to-end tests for all outcomes in both human and json modes.
- Early refusal path where old context exists and new parse fails.
- Exit-code and stream routing behavior remains unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Orchestrator selects json and human renderers deterministically from output mode.\n2) Final payload rendering preserves upstream check and refusal semantics.\n3) Integration tests cover compatible, incompatible, and refusal output handoff paths.

title: Implement exit-code mapping and output stream routing
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement outcome-to-exit mapping (0/1/2) and output-stream routing rules (`stdout`/`stderr`) exactly as specified for human vs JSON modes.

Deliverables:
- `Outcome`, `OutputMode`, and `OutputStream` enums finalized.
- `exit_code()` and `output_stream()` helpers implemented and reused by `run()`.
- Main entrypoint preserves process-level failure behavior.

Definition of Done:
- Mapping is deterministic and test-covered.
- Human refusal routes to stderr; JSON always routes to stdout.
- No ad-hoc stream writes bypass routing helpers.

Test Cases:
- Unit tests for every outcome/mode stream combination.
- Integration tests asserting stdout/stderr placement for refusal vs non-refusal.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Exit code mapping is exactly 0, 1, and 2 for compatible, incompatible, and refusal outcomes.\n2) Human-mode refusal writes to stderr while non-refusal writes to stdout.\n3) JSON mode writes all domain outcomes to stdout.

title: Create fixture corpus for all required structural scenarios
description:
Implementation Scope:
Create and document fixture files listed in PLAN under `tests/fixtures`, including compatible, schema drift, type shift, duplicate key, empty/header-only, and no-header cases.

Deliverables:
- Fixture files committed with predictable small datasets.
- Fixture naming consistent with PLAN.
- Fixture README describing purpose of each pair/file.

Definition of Done:
- Every planned scenario has at least one deterministic fixture.
- Fixtures are minimal but sufficient for parser/check/output tests.
- No fixture relies on randomness or environment-specific behavior.

Test Cases:
- Fixture sanity tests confirming files are readable and expected row counts exist.
- Smoke runs proving each fixture can trigger intended outcome.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Required fixture set exists under tests fixtures with documented purpose.\n2) Fixtures are deterministic, minimal, and readable in tests.\n3) Each fixture maps to at least one intended scenario in the test matrix.

title: Final PLAN conformance audit and one-shot readiness signoff
description:
Implementation Scope:
Run a final conformance pass against `docs/PLAN.md`, verify each must-have requirement is implemented/tested, and produce signoff criteria for five-agent one-shot execution.

Deliverables:
- Requirement-by-requirement checklist mapped to code/tests.
- Gap list resolved or explicitly deferred per PLAN "Can defer" section.
- Final ready-to-execute summary for parallel agent pickup.

Definition of Done:
- Every v0.1 must-have item is marked complete with evidence.
- Deferred items are documented as intentionally out-of-scope.
- Team can run one-shot implementation with no ambiguity.

Test Cases:
- Checklist review against CLI flags, refusal set, output modes, and exit codes.
- Confirmation that all blocking dependencies are closed.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Final checklist confirms every v0.1 must-have is implemented and tested.\n2) Deferred items are explicitly documented as out-of-scope for this release.\n3) Readiness signoff references passing quality gates and dependency closure.

title: Add scan/check unit tests for check correctness and reasoning
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement unit tests for scanner outputs, check evaluations, coverage math, nullability behavior for missing keys, type-shift detection, and reason-generation templates.

Deliverables:
- Scanner-focused tests for row/key/type accumulation.
- Check-suite tests for pass/fail thresholds.
- Reason-template exact-string tests.

Definition of Done:
- All check logic is validated independently of renderer code.
- Edge cases (all-missing columns, missing key, mixed failures) are covered.
- Deterministic reason ordering is verified.

Test Cases:
- Key coverage math with asymmetric key sets.
- Type shifts Numeric->NonNumeric and NonNumeric->Numeric.
- Multi-failure suite test with expected reasons array.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Scanner and check tests cover core math and nullability edge cases.\n2) Type-shift and key-coverage edge scenarios are verified.\n3) Reason-template correctness is asserted with exact-string checks.

title: Wire end-to-end integration harness for golden suites
description:
Implementation Scope:
Own integration harness wiring after mode-specific golden tests are added. This issue verifies CLI-level end-to-end behavior with representative fixtures and ensures both JSON and human golden suites execute through the same invocation path.

Deliverables:
- Shared integration runner/utilities for invoking `shape` in test mode.
- Cross-mode smoke tests that execute golden suites together.
- Stability checks around path echoing, exit codes, and context propagation.

Definition of Done:
- Harness runs both JSON and human golden suites without duplication.
- End-to-end CLI tests validate combined behavior across core outcomes.
- Integration tests are deterministic and CI-safe.

Test Cases:
- CLI E2E runs for COMPATIBLE/INCOMPATIBLE/REFUSAL using harness utilities.
- Exit-code assertions (0/1/2) across representative scenarios.
- Dialect/context propagation checks on early refusal paths.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Integration harness executes JSON and human golden suites through shared CLI helpers.\n2) End-to-end tests assert exit codes 0, 1, and 2 on representative fixtures.\n3) Harness avoids duplicate invocation logic across test modules.

title: Add JSON golden snapshots for shape.v0 outcomes
description:
Implementation Scope:
Split integration golden coverage by output mode. This issue owns JSON snapshot baselines for COMPATIBLE/INCOMPATIBLE/REFUSAL with strict key presence and nullability assertions.

Deliverables:
- JSON snapshot fixtures for key/no-key compatible runs, incompatible runs, and refusals.
- Snapshot assertions for field presence, nullability, and reason/refusal blocks.
- Regression test harness for stable `shape.v0` schema output.

Definition of Done:
- JSON snapshots are deterministic and CI-stable.
- Top-level keys are always present per PLAN (no omissions).
- Snapshot updates are intentional and reviewed.

Test Cases:
- Compatible with key snapshot.
- Compatible without key snapshot.
- Incompatible and refusal snapshots including partial dialect context.
acceptance_criteria: 1) JSON snapshots exist for key and no-key compatible, incompatible, and refusal outcomes.\n2) Snapshots assert field presence and nullability invariants.\n3) Golden tests are deterministic and CI-stable.

title: shape v0 epic: scan and structural checks lane
description:
Objective:
Build the single-pass scan engine and the four structural checks: schema overlap, key viability, row granularity, and type consistency.

Deliverables:
- Implement and land all lane:scan issues.
- Enforce deterministic check results and reason generation.

Definition of Done:
- One-pass scanner computes row/key/type stats without record materialization.
- Check results and reason templates match PLAN exactly.
- Outcome determination logic is complete and test-backed.

Test Cases:
- Unit tests for each check and edge cases (missing key, duplicates, type shifts).
- Multi-check incompatibility accumulation tests.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All lane scan child issues are closed.\n2) Check computations and outcome logic are validated by unit tests.\n3) Lane outputs are stable for renderer consumers.

title: Implement CLI args, flag validation, and deferred flag shape
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement clap parsing for positional args and flags in PLAN v0.1, including current flags (`--key`, `--delimiter`, `--json`, `--describe`, `--version`) and deferred-shape flags (`--profile`, `--profile-id`, `--lock`, `--max-rows`, `--max-bytes`) with correct validation semantics.

Deliverables:
- Args parser with mutual exclusivity for `--profile` and `--profile-id`.
- Correct command behavior when `--describe` is passed without positional files.
- Validation errors mapped to refusal/CLI error behavior (exit 2).

Definition of Done:
- CLI parser accepts valid combinations and rejects invalid ones.
- Deferred flags are represented in args contracts for schema stability.
- Parsing behavior matches PLAN wording and expected precedence.

Test Cases:
- Parse success tests for happy-path invocations.
- Parse failure tests for ambiguous profile selectors and malformed limits.
- `--describe` no-arg invocation test.
acceptance_criteria: 1) CLI parser accepts all required v0.1 flags and positional arguments.\n2) Invalid flag combinations, including profile selector ambiguity, fail deterministically.\n3) shape --describe succeeds without positional file arguments.

title: Implement blank-record semantics and E_EMPTY pre/post-scan guards
description:
Implementation Scope:
Split blank-record and emptiness logic into a dedicated task: blank row detection, step-11 pre-scan emptiness guard, and step-16 post-scan emptiness guard for all-blank datasets.

Deliverables:
- Shared `is_blank_record` helper and consistent usage points.
- Pre-scan and post-scan `E_EMPTY` decisions wired per PLAN.
- Refusal detail payload correctness for affected file/row count.

Definition of Done:
- Header-only files and all-blank-data files both reliably trigger `E_EMPTY`.
- Row-count behavior excludes blank records consistently.
- Guard sequencing matches PLAN execution notes.

Test Cases:
- Header-only fixture triggers `E_EMPTY` at pre/post stage as applicable.
- Files with only blank records trigger post-scan `E_EMPTY`.
- Mixed files with at least one non-blank row do not trigger `E_EMPTY`.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Blank records are skipped consistently before row counting.\n2) Header-only and all-blank datasets trigger E_EMPTY at the proper guard stage.\n3) Non-blank datasets do not produce false-positive E_EMPTY refusals.

title: Run post-integration regression hardening pass
description:
Implementation Scope:
Run behavioral hardening after the integrated build is compiling. Focus on regressions surfaced by integration and tighten tests around fixed defects. This issue no longer owns first-pass compile/interface reconciliation.

Deliverables:
- Investigate and fix integration regressions found after compile-green state.
- Add or refine tests for each fixed regression to prevent recurrence.
- Confirm hardened behavior before final quality/signoff tasks.

Definition of Done:
- Regressions identified during integrated test runs are resolved.
- Each fixed regression has targeted test coverage.
- Integration hardening results are stable for quality-gate execution.

Test Cases:
- Run focused regression scenarios from integration suites.
- Validate fixed cases in both json and human output paths.
- Re-run representative end-to-end cases after fixes.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Regression issues found after compile-green integration are fixed.\n2) Each fixed regression is covered by a targeted test.\n3) Hardened behavior is stable for final quality-gate execution.

title: Add refusal schema unit tests
description:
Implementation Scope:
Own refusal-payload and refusal-schema unit tests separately from parser contract tests. This keeps parser tests focused and allows refusal-schema verification to progress independently.

Deliverables:
- Unit tests for refusal envelope fields (`code`, `message`, `detail`, `next_command`).
- Per-code detail-shape assertions for shipped refusal codes.
- Actionable vs non-actionable `next_command` behavior coverage.

Definition of Done:
- Refusal schema tests pass for all shipped refusal codes.
- Tests validate structure and deterministic key presence, not only free-form strings.
- Regression tests exist for at least one actionable and one non-actionable refusal.

Test Cases:
- `E_DIALECT` includes candidates and non-null `next_command`.
- `E_IO` and `E_EMPTY` include expected detail fields with null `next_command` where appropriate.
- Code serialization remains stable and uppercase.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Refusal unit tests validate envelope shape and per-code detail structure for shipped codes.\n2) Actionable and non-actionable next_command behavior is explicitly asserted.\n3) Refusal code serialization remains stable and uppercase in tests.

title: Implement header normalization and duplicate-header refusal (E_HEADERS)
description:
Implementation Scope:
Extract and implement header-specific behavior from the broader parser work: ASCII-trim normalization, empty-name replacement to `__shape_col_<N>`, uniqueness checks, and `E_HEADERS` refusal details.

Deliverables:
- Header normalization utility aligned to PLAN/rvl parity.
- Duplicate-header detection with encoded offending name in refusal detail.
- Unit tests for normalization edge cases and duplicate collision rules.

Definition of Done:
- Header normalization behavior is deterministic and reusable across old/new file parsing.
- Duplicate or missing-header scenarios emit correct `E_HEADERS` payloads.
- All header normalization tests pass.

Test Cases:
- Headers with leading/trailing ASCII space/tab normalize correctly.
- Empty header names map to stable `__shape_col_<N>` placeholders.
- Duplicate normalized names trigger `E_HEADERS` with encoded `name` field.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Header normalization applies ASCII trim and empty-name substitution correctly.\n2) Duplicate normalized headers refuse with E_HEADERS and encoded duplicate name.\n3) Header unit tests cover trim, empty, and duplicate collision cases.

title: Implement single-pass scan engine with key and column tracking
description:
Implementation Scope:
Build the performance-critical scanner that performs row counting, key scan (distinct/duplicate/empty), and per-column numeric classification in one pass per file.

Deliverables:
- `scan_file()` implementation with streaming ByteRecord iteration.
- `KeyScan` accumulation and memory-safe set usage.
- `NumericClassifier` with early termination when non-numeric state is reached.

Definition of Done:
- Exactly one row-pass per file for scan metrics.
- No full-record collection into vectors.
- Scan outputs are directly consumable by check evaluators.

Test Cases:
- Row-count and key-count correctness on small fixtures.
- Duplicate and empty-key accumulation tests.
- Column classification tests for Numeric/NonNumeric/AllMissing.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Scanner performs one pass per file while collecting row, key, and type data.\n2) Key duplicate and empty counts plus distinct-value tracking are correct.\n3) Column classifications output numeric, non-numeric, and all-missing deterministically.

title: Run quality gates and publish acceptance checklist
description:
Implementation Scope:
Execute project quality gates and produce an acceptance checklist proving PLAN compliance across CLI, parser, checks, outputs, refusals, and exit semantics.

Deliverables:
- Passing `cargo fmt --check`.
- Passing `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`.
- Passing `cargo test` with full suite.
- A concise acceptance checklist artifact in repo docs or test notes.

Definition of Done:
- All three quality gate commands pass without local patches pending.
- Any discovered defects are tracked/fixed via beads before close.
- Acceptance checklist maps directly to PLAN must-have scope.

Test Cases:
- Run and capture output of all quality gate commands.
- Verify acceptance checklist includes each required refusal code and output mode.
acceptance_criteria: 1) cargo fmt check, cargo clippy with denied warnings, and cargo test all pass.\n2) Acceptance checklist maps each v0.1 must-have item to evidence.\n3) Remaining gaps are filed as follow-up issues before close.

title: Assemble unified check suite and nullability contract
description:
Implementation Scope:
Compose the split check modules into the final `CheckSuite` contract consumed by orchestrator/renderers. This issue assembles outputs, enforces nullability policy, and standardizes check status semantics.

Deliverables:
- Unified `CheckSuite` assembly from schema/key and row/type sub-check outputs.
- Nullability handling for key-absent / key-not-found scenarios.
- Stable check-field ordering and naming for renderers.

Definition of Done:
- `CheckSuite` contains all required check blocks for non-refusal outcomes.
- Null/Some behavior across key-derived fields matches PLAN exactly.
- Downstream outcome/reason/rendering code can consume suite without ad-hoc transformations.

Test Cases:
- Suite assembly tests for with-key, no-key, and missing-key variants.
- Nullability assertions on all optional fields.
- End-to-end compatibility/incompatibility scenarios using assembled suite.
acceptance_criteria: 1) CheckSuite includes all required check blocks for non-refusal outcomes.\n2) Optional fields are null or populated exactly per PLAN nullability rules.\n3) Suite contract is stable for both renderers.

title: Implement refusal code system and detail payload builders
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement refusal code enum and payload/detail builders for all documented codes (including deferred ones for schema stability) with message/next-step generation rules.

Deliverables:
- `RefusalCode` enum and serialization mapping.
- Detail builders for each code schema in PLAN.
- `next_command` builder for actionable cases (`E_DIALECT`, `E_TOO_LARGE`) and `null` otherwise.

Definition of Done:
- Refusal payload shape is consistent across pipeline call sites.
- Deferred-code schemas are representable even if not yet triggerable.
- Messages and details are concise, actionable, and test-covered.

Test Cases:
- Unit tests for each code’s detail object fields.
- Actionable vs non-actionable `next_command` behavior tests.
- Serialization tests for uppercase code names.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Refusal payload schema is consistent across all refusal-producing paths.\n2) Documented refusal codes serialize to expected uppercase identifiers.\n3) Actionable refusals include next_command while non-actionable refusals set it to null.

title: Implement JSON renderer for shape.v0 schema and nullability
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement JSON rendering for COMPATIBLE/INCOMPATIBLE/REFUSAL ensuring full top-level key presence, per-field nullability rules, exact structure for checks/refusal blocks, and full-precision float emission.

Deliverables:
- Serializable output structs for all outcomes.
- Complete field emission with no omitted keys.
- `files.old/new` values echoed exactly as CLI input strings.

Definition of Done:
- JSON output validates against PLAN examples and nullability matrix.
- `checks`/`reasons` nullability differs correctly by outcome.
- Dialect block includes partial context on early refusal when available.

Test Cases:
- Snapshot tests for compatible with key, compatible without key, incompatible, refusal.
- Nullability tests for key-related fields when key missing/absent.
- Float precision test to ensure no forced rounding.
acceptance_criteria: 1) JSON output always emits complete top-level shape.v0 keys.\n2) Nullability behavior matches PLAN for key, profile, verification, checks, reasons, and refusal fields.\n3) Snapshot tests verify compatible, incompatible, and refusal structures.

title: shape v0 epic: fixtures, tests, and acceptance lane
description:
Objective:
Create the full fixture/test matrix and acceptance harness needed to one-shot implementation quality across all lanes.

Deliverables:
- Implement and land all lane:tests issues.
- Cover core functionality, refusal matrix, outputs, and exit codes.

Definition of Done:
- Fixture corpus exists and is documented.
- Unit and integration suites cover all major PLAN contracts.
- Project passes `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test`.

Test Cases:
- Module-level tests for parsing/check logic.
- Golden-output and E2E exit-code tests.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All lane tests child issues are closed.\n2) Fixture corpus and test matrix cover v0.1 must-have scope.\n3) Quality gates pass before lane closure.

title: shape v0 epic: output rendering and refusal schema lane
description:
Objective:
Implement strict human and JSON output contracts, including refusal payload schemas, identifier encoding, number formatting, and stream routing behavior.

Deliverables:
- Implement and land all lane:output issues.
- Preserve exact field presence/nullability and formatting requirements.

Definition of Done:
- JSON output always emits complete top-level schema.
- Human output matches PLAN layout and wording conventions.
- Refusal envelope and detail objects are stable and complete.

Test Cases:
- Golden tests for COMPATIBLE/INCOMPATIBLE/REFUSAL in JSON and human modes.
- Stream routing tests for human refusal to stderr and JSON to stdout.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All lane output child issues are closed.\n2) JSON and human renderers pass golden snapshot suites.\n3) Refusal payload and routing behavior is validated.

title: Update README/operator docs to match shipped v0 behavior
description:
Implementation Scope:
Align `README.md` and operator-facing docs with final implementation details: CLI usage, outcomes, refusal behavior, and examples consistent with PLAN and shipped behavior.

Deliverables:
- README updates for v0 command usage and outputs.
- Operator contract references verified for `--describe` output.
- Any stale statements corrected.

Definition of Done:
- Documentation is accurate against tested binary behavior.
- Example commands and outputs are executable and current.
- No contradiction between docs and tests.

Test Cases:
- Manual doc example smoke-run validation.
- Spot-check output snippets against golden tests.
acceptance_criteria: 1) README and operator docs reflect actual CLI, output, and refusal behavior.\n2) Documented commands and examples execute successfully.\n3) Documentation does not contradict snapshot-backed behavior.

title: Add parser contract unit tests for CSV behavior
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement parser-focused unit tests covering input and encoding guard behavior, delimiter parsing, sep directive behavior, dialect detection edges, and header normalization. Refusal-schema tests are split into a dedicated issue.

Deliverables:
- Focused parser tests in module-level test files.
- Coverage for delimiter precedence and dialect edge behavior.
- Header and blank-record parser-path checks.

Definition of Done:
- Parser unit tests pass reliably.
- Tests cover positive parse behavior and parser-origin refusal paths.
- Parser behavior remains stable under delimiter and sep precedence permutations.

Test Cases:
- E_IO, E_ENCODING, E_CSV_PARSE, E_HEADERS, E_DIALECT, E_EMPTY parser-path trigger checks.
- Delimiter flag precedence over sep checks.
- Duplicate-header and ambiguous-dialect fixture tests.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Parser-focused tests pass across input, delimiter, dialect, header, and blank-record behavior.\n2) Delimiter and sep precedence behavior is explicitly covered.\n3) Parser-origin refusal paths are validated without relying on renderer snapshots.

title: Implement core fail-fast orchestrator pipeline flow
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement the core orchestrator pipeline in PLAN order through check evaluation and outcome determination. This issue now excludes final renderer handoff (split to dedicated integration issue).

Deliverables:
- Execute staged old/new input processing with fail-fast refusal semantics.
- Carry parsed context forward for downstream rendering and refusal reporting.
- Produce stable domain-level pipeline result consumed by output handoff.

Definition of Done:
- Pipeline execution order and stop conditions match PLAN step sequence.
- First fatal refusal terminates processing without unnecessary downstream work.
- Domain result supports compatible, incompatible, and refusal paths.

Test Cases:
- Fail-fast tests where old input fails before new is read.
- Tests where old parses and new fails while preserving old context.
- Happy-path domain flow tests with key and no-key execution.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Core pipeline sequencing matches PLAN fail-fast behavior through outcome determination.\n2) Domain-level results are produced for compatible, incompatible, and refusal cases.\n3) Renderer handoff is excluded and delegated to its dedicated integration issue.

title: Implement missing-token and numeric classification helpers
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement `is_missing()` and `parses_as_numeric()` helpers with grammar/parsing coverage required for type classification, including currency, thousands separators, scientific notation, and accounting negatives.

Deliverables:
- Missing-token detector (case-insensitive) for PLAN token set.
- Numeric classifier predicate returning bool (no f64 materialization needed).
- Utilities documented for scanner integration.

Definition of Done:
- Numeric grammar matches PLAN/rvl conventions.
- Missing tokens are excluded from numeric/non-numeric decisions.
- Helpers are deterministic and unit-tested with edge inputs.

Test Cases:
- Positive numeric samples (`123`, `-123.45`, `1e6`, `$1,234.56`, `(123.45)`).
- Negative numeric samples (`12-3`, malformed commas, text).
- Missing token cases including empty, `-`, `NA`, `NULL`, `NAN`, `NONE`.
acceptance_criteria: 1) is_missing recognizes the PLAN missing-token set case-insensitively.\n2) parses_as_numeric accepts PLAN numeric grammar and rejects malformed values.\n3) Unit tests cover currency, thousands, scientific, and accounting forms.

title: Implement row-granularity and type-consistency checks
description:
Implementation Scope:
Split check implementation to isolate row/type logic: row counts and key overlap metrics, plus type-shift detection between numeric and non-numeric states with AllMissing compatibility.

Deliverables:
- `row_granularity` and `type_consistency` check modules implemented.
- Type shift detection with column-level payloads (`old_type`, `new_type`).
- Numeric-column counting and informational row-granularity semantics.

Definition of Done:
- Row-granularity always reports `status=pass` and correct count fields.
- Type consistency fails only on Numeric<->NonNumeric shifts.
- AllMissing handling never creates false-positive type shifts.

Test Cases:
- Row-only count reporting with and without key context.
- Numeric -> non-numeric and non-numeric -> numeric shifts.
- AllMissing columns in one/both files remain compatible.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Row granularity always reports pass with correct row and key metrics.\n2) Type consistency fails only on numeric and non-numeric shifts.\n3) All-missing columns never trigger type-shift failures.

title: Implement delimiter flag parsing and sep= directive precedence
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement delimiter argument parser and per-file `sep=` directive parsing with exact acceptance/rejection rules and precedence (`--delimiter` overrides `sep=`).

Deliverables:
- Parser for named delimiters, hex form, and single-char literals.
- Validation rejections for disallowed bytes (`0x00`, `0x0A`, `0x0D`, `0x22`, >=`0x7F`).
- `sep=` parser that consumes first line only when exact-match form applies.

Definition of Done:
- Delimiter parsing is deterministic and case-insensitive where required.
- Old/new files can independently resolve their own `sep=` directive.
- Override semantics are test-covered.

Test Cases:
- Positive tests for `comma/tab/semicolon/pipe/caret`, `0x2c`, and literal delimiters.
- Negative tests for invalid hex and disallowed characters.
- `sep=` consumed vs not consumed edge-case tests.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Delimiter parser accepts named, hex, and single-char forms per PLAN.\n2) Invalid delimiter values are rejected deterministically.\n3) Delimiter flag precedence over sep directive is test-covered.

title: Implement dialect auto-detection scoring and refusal behavior
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement dialect auto-detection with candidate ordering, RFC/backslash probing, histogram scoring tuple comparison, deterministic tie-breaks, and `E_DIALECT` refusal conditions.

Deliverables:
- Candidate evaluation for `,`, `\t`, `;`, `|`, `^`.
- Lexicographic scoring `(records_parsed, mode_count, mode_fields)`.
- Ambiguity handling for equal-score/different-sample scenarios and one-column refusal guard.

Definition of Done:
- Detection algorithm matches PLAN semantics exactly.
- Deterministic winner for strict-best and identical-score/same-sample ties.
- Refusal payload includes candidates and suggested `next_command` when actionable.

Test Cases:
- Auto-detect positive tests for each candidate delimiter.
- Ambiguous dialect fixture triggers `E_DIALECT`.
- One-column winning delimiter case triggers `E_DIALECT`.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Auto-detect evaluates delimiters in PLAN order with correct scoring tuple.\n2) Ambiguous detection and one-column outcomes refuse with E_DIALECT.\n3) Tie-break behavior is deterministic and test-covered.

title: Implement file reads and encoding guards (E_IO/E_ENCODING)
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement byte ingestion and encoding guard logic with refusal mapping: read errors -> `E_IO`, unsupported BOM/NUL detection -> `E_ENCODING`, UTF-8 BOM stripping behavior per PLAN.

Deliverables:
- Input loader returning raw bytes and metadata needed downstream.
- Guard function enforcing UTF-16/32 and NUL detection policy.
- Refusal detail payloads populated with file path and issue subtype.

Definition of Done:
- Guard order matches PLAN (UTF-32 before UTF-16, then UTF-8 strip, then NUL scan).
- Refusal payload details are exact and actionable.
- Behavior is identical for old/new files.

Test Cases:
- Fixtures with UTF-16/32 BOMs and NUL bytes trigger `E_ENCODING`.
- Missing path or permission issue triggers `E_IO`.
- UTF-8 BOM input parses successfully.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Missing or unreadable file maps to E_IO with file-specific detail.\n2) UTF-16 or UTF-32 BOM and NUL byte inputs map to E_ENCODING with correct issue subtype.\n3) UTF-8 BOM is stripped and parsing continues.

title: shape v0 epic: CLI and orchestrator lane
description:
Objective:
Own all CLI contract and orchestration plumbing defined in `docs/PLAN.md`, including fail-fast sequencing and output routing behavior.

Deliverables:
- Implement and land all lane:cli issues.
- Keep module boundaries aligned to the PLAN module map.
- Ensure behavior parity with PLAN execution flow and exit semantics.

Definition of Done:
- All lane:cli children are closed.
- `shape --help`, `shape --version`, and `shape --describe` behaviors match PLAN.
- Orchestrator path returns deterministic 0/1/2 outcomes.

Test Cases:
- CLI smoke checks for required/optional flags.
- Invalid args return exit code 2.
- Orchestrator fail-fast order validated by integration tests.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All lane cli child issues are closed.\n2) CLI and orchestrator behavior matches PLAN and integration tests.\n3) Lane exits with no unresolved blockers.

title: Implement core CSV row parser and record iteration primitives
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement the parser core that turns selected dialect settings into streamed CSV byte-record iteration. This issue now excludes header-normalization and blank/E_EMPTY policy work (split into dedicated issues).

Deliverables:
- ByteRecord streaming parser over selected delimiter/escape mode.
- RFC4180 parse path with documented fallback expectations.
- Reusable parser entrypoints for old/new input scans.

Definition of Done:
- Parser can iterate records without materializing whole-row vectors.
- Parsing errors surface as `E_CSV_PARSE` with usable detail payloads.
- APIs are stable for scanner/check/orchestrator callers.

Test Cases:
- Successful parsing on canonical comma/tab fixtures.
- Malformed row fixture triggers `E_CSV_PARSE` with line/error metadata.
- Large fixture smoke test confirms streaming iteration behavior.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Parser streams byte records without full-row materialization.\n2) Parse failures map to E_CSV_PARSE with actionable detail fields.\n3) Parser entrypoints are reusable by scanner and orchestrator.

title: Add human-output and stream-routing golden tests
description:
Implementation Scope:
Split integration golden coverage for human mode and stream routing behavior. Own fixed-layout text snapshots and stdout/stderr routing assertions.

Deliverables:
- Human output snapshots for COMPATIBLE/INCOMPATIBLE/REFUSAL.
- Routing assertions: human refusal to stderr; JSON outcomes to stdout.
- Number-format and section-presence checks in human output.

Definition of Done:
- Human snapshots match PLAN layout/terminology precisely.
- Stream routing behavior is verified via integration tests.
- Key/no-key conditional section behavior is covered.

Test Cases:
- Compatible with key and without key human snapshots.
- Incompatible snapshots with reasons and detail lines.
- Refusal snapshot and stream assertions.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Human snapshots exist for compatible, incompatible, and refusal outcomes.\n2) Stream routing behavior is asserted for stderr and stdout expectations.\n3) Key-conditional sections are verified in snapshots.

title: Implement schema-overlap and key-viability checks
description:
Implementation Scope:
Split check implementation to isolate schema and key logic: compute common/old-only/new-only columns, overlap ratio, key existence/uniqueness/empty rules, and key coverage semantics.

Deliverables:
- `schema_overlap` and `key_viability` check modules implemented.
- Key-not-found behavior produces check failure (INCOMPATIBLE) not refusal.
- Coverage/nullability behavior matches PLAN.

Definition of Done:
- Schema and key checks can be executed independently from row/type checks.
- Key check outputs (`found_*`, `unique_*`, `coverage`) follow nullability rules exactly.
- Reasons for key failures are emitted with exact templates.

Test Cases:
- Zero/partial/full overlap schema scenarios.
- Key missing in old/new, duplicates, and empty-key value scenarios.
- Coverage calculations including empty key sets.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Schema overlap fields for common, old-only, new-only, and ratio are correct.\n2) Key viability handles found, missing, duplicate, and empty cases with correct nullability.\n3) Key-not-found behavior is incompatible check failure, not refusal.

title: Implement outcome determination and exact reason templates
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement final compatibility decision logic and reason string generation templates exactly as specified in PLAN for all incompatible scenarios.

Deliverables:
- `determine_outcome()` helper using check statuses.
- Reason builder with exact wording templates and substitution fields.
- Multi-reason accumulation in deterministic order.

Definition of Done:
- Outcome logic ignores row_granularity failures (always informational).
- Reason strings match template text exactly (including punctuation/casing).
- Multiple simultaneous failures are preserved in output.

Test Cases:
- Unit tests for each reason template format.
- Multi-failure scenario test for deterministic ordering.
- Compatible run test ensures empty reason array.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Outcome decision logic ignores informational row granularity status.\n2) Reason strings match PLAN templates exactly.\n3) Multi-failure runs emit deterministic reason ordering.

title: shape v0 epic: CSV parsing and input contract lane
description:
Objective:
Implement the full CSV input contract with rvl-parity behavior: encoding guard, delimiter handling, `sep=` directive, parse/refusal semantics, and header normalization.

Deliverables:
- Implement and land all lane:csv issues.
- Match PLAN refusal triggers for `E_IO`, `E_ENCODING`, `E_CSV_PARSE`, `E_HEADERS`, `E_DIALECT`, `E_EMPTY`.

Definition of Done:
- CSV ingestion path is deterministic and fully covered by tests.
- Delimiter behavior matches PLAN precedence and tie-break rules.
- Header normalization and duplicate detection are stable.

Test Cases:
- Fixture-driven delimiter, `sep=`, BOM, parse failure, and duplicate-header cases.
- Ambiguous dialect and single-column dialector refusal checks.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All lane csv child issues are closed.\n2) CSV contract behavior matches PLAN including refusal semantics.\n3) Lane behavior is validated by parser and refusal tests.
